evals.prompt:
I collected a diagnostic bundle with infrahub-collect after our
proposed-change UI started throwing 500s. Analyze these bundle excerpts and
write a findings report, including how you would check whether this is a
known Infrahub issue. Do NOT actually run any commands; include the exact
commands you would run in the report.

bundle/bundle_information.json:
{"deployment": {"type": "docker-compose", "project": "infrahub-prod"},
 "collectors": [
   {"name": "logs/server", "status": "ok"},
   {"name": "logs/database", "status": "ok"},
   {"name": "logs/message-queue", "status": "failed", "error": "container not running"}]}

bundle/logs/server/infrahub-server-1.log:
2026-07-20 09:14:52 ERROR infrahub.graphql Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/lib/python3.12/site-packages/infrahub/graphql/mutations/proposed_change.py", line 142, in mutate
  File "/usr/lib/python3.12/site-packages/infrahub/core/registry.py", line 88, in get_schema
infrahub.exceptions.SchemaNotFoundError: schema 'CoreProposedChange' not found on branch 'atl-fix-vlans-0f3aa9c2'
evals.expected_output: A findings report that reads the manifest first (flagging the failed message-queue collector as a finding), cites bundle paths as evidence for the SchemaNotFoundError traceback, proposes a `gh search issues --repo opsmill/infrahub --state all` query built from stable keys (exception class + schema kind, branch name stripped), stays read-only, and hands filing off to infrahub-reporting-issues.
expectations: Reads bundle_information.json first and treats the failed message-queue collector as a finding
Cites bundle/logs/... paths as evidence for each finding
Searches opsmill/infrahub issues with `gh search issues --state all`
Search keys keep SchemaNotFoundError but strip the volatile branch name
Recommends no deployment-mutating commands and does not run `gh issue create`

assertions.name: mentions-manifest
assertions.check: Report references bundle_information.json / the manifest

assertions.name: cites-bundle-evidence
assertions.check: Findings cite bundle/<service>/ file paths as evidence

assertions.name: github-search
assertions.check: Report includes `gh search issues` against opsmill/infrahub

assertions.name: search-state-all
assertions.check: Issue search uses `--state all` to cover closed issues

assertions.name: search-keyword
assertions.check: Search keys include the stable exception class SchemaNotFoundError

assertions.name: search-excludes-token
assertions.check: Search keys strip the volatile branch suffix 0f3aa9c2

assertions.name: no-mutating-commands
assertions.check: Report includes no restart/delete/down commands

assertions.name: no-direct-issue-filing
assertions.check: Report does not run `gh issue create`

assertions.name: cross-link-reporting-issues
assertions.check: Report hands filing off to infrahub-reporting-issues

evals.prompt:
Our Docker Compose Infrahub deployment fell over yesterday and I
collected a diagnostic bundle. Here is what a sweep of the bundle shows.
Correlate these signals and write a findings report. Do NOT actually run
any commands.

bundle/bundle_information.json: all collectors succeeded (docker-compose
project infrahub-prod, collected 2026-07-18 14:11).

bundle/logs/database/database-1.log:
2026-07-18 14:02:11.408+0000 ERROR ... java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space

bundle/logs/server/infrahub-server-1.log (41 similar lines between 14:02:19 and 14:05:40):
2026-07-18 14:02:19 ERROR infrahub.database Unable to connect to database: Connection refused

bundle/logs/task-worker/ contains task-worker-1.log and task-worker-1.previous.log;
tail of task-worker-1.previous.log:
2026-07-18 14:03:05 ERROR ... database connection timeout (attempt 12/12)
2026-07-18 14:03:07 CRITICAL worker shutting down
evals.expected_output: A findings report that groups the database OOM, the server connection errors, and the task-worker restart into one incident on a shared timeline, names the database OOM as the root and the rest as cascade, treats task-worker-1.previous.log as restart evidence, cites bundle paths for every claim, and recommends no restarts or other mutations.
expectations: References the manifest before the logs
Groups all three services' signals into a single correlated incident
Distinguishes the database OOM root from the cascade errors downstream
Treats task-worker-1.previous.log as evidence the worker restarted
Cites bundle/logs/... paths with timestamps for each signal

assertions.name: mentions-manifest
assertions.check: Report references bundle_information.json / the manifest

assertions.name: cites-bundle-evidence
assertions.check: Findings cite bundle/<service>/ file paths as evidence

assertions.name: restart-evidence
assertions.check: Report treats the .previous.log file as restart/crash evidence

assertions.name: incident-grouping
assertions.check: Signals are correlated into an incident with root vs cascade

assertions.name: mentions-all
assertions.check: Report covers database, server, task-worker, and the 14:02 window

assertions.name: no-mutating-commands
assertions.check: Report includes no restart/delete/down commands

evals.prompt:
Our task-workers will not start after an upgrade. In the diagnostic
bundle I collected, every worker log ends with the traceback below. Is this
already a known Infrahub issue, and what should I do next? Do NOT actually
run any commands; write the exact commands and steps as a short report.

bundle/logs/task-worker/task-worker-1.log:
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.12/site-packages/infrahub/workers/infrahub_async.py", line 67, in setup
infrahub.exceptions.InitializationError: Unable to connect to the message broker at rabbitmq-7f9c4d.acme.net:5672
evals.expected_output: A report that cites the bundle path for the traceback, builds the GitHub search from stable keys (InitializationError + message-broker wording, the internal hostname stripped), searches opsmill/infrahub with `gh search issues --state all`, does not file anything itself, and hands any filing or commenting off to infrahub-reporting-issues.
expectations: Cites bundle/logs/task-worker/... as evidence for the traceback
Searches opsmill/infrahub issues with `gh search issues --state all`
Search keys keep InitializationError but strip the internal broker hostname
Does not run `gh issue create` or any deployment-mutating command
Cross-links infrahub-reporting-issues for filing or commenting

assertions.name: cites-bundle-evidence
assertions.check: Report cites bundle/<service>/ file paths as evidence

assertions.name: github-search
assertions.check: Report includes `gh search issues` against opsmill/infrahub

assertions.name: search-state-all
assertions.check: Issue search uses `--state all` to cover closed issues

assertions.name: search-keyword
assertions.check: Search keys include the stable exception class InitializationError

assertions.name: search-excludes-token
assertions.check: Search keys strip the internal hostname (acme)

assertions.name: no-direct-issue-filing
assertions.check: Report does not run `gh issue create`

assertions.name: cross-link-reporting-issues
assertions.check: Report hands filing off to infrahub-reporting-issues

assertions.name: no-mutating-commands
assertions.check: Report includes no restart/delete/down commands
